gdp.source: INEGI, Producto Interno Bruto por Entidad Federativa (PIBE), base 2018, valores corrientes (PIB a precios de mercado). Per-state figures from INEGI's per-entity PIBE 2023 boletines (Comunicado 752/24 etc., 6-Dec-2024).
gdp.unit: MXN billions (nominal); 1 billion = 1,000 million pesos. Sum of 32 states = 31,855.566 bn = the INEGI national PIB total.

gdp_per_capita.source: Computed = INEGI PIBE 2023 nominal GDP / CONAPO mid-2023 population.
gdp_per_capita.fx_note: USD = MXN / 17.5 (fixed rate per instructions, NOT a market rate). National reference: 242,921 MXN ~= $13,881 USD.

population.source: CONAPO, Proyecciones de la Poblacion de Mexico y de las Entidades Federativas 2020-2070 (conciliacion demografica, base Censo 2020), poblacion a mitad de ano. File: 00_Pob_Mitad_1950_2070.csv.

poverty.source: CONEVAL, Medicion multidimensional de la pobreza 2022 (ENIGH 2022), % de poblacion en situacion de pobreza. File: Anexo estadistico entidades 2022.xlsx.

informality.source: INEGI, ENOE, Tasa de Informalidad Laboral (TIL1) por entidad federativa (Boletin de Indicador 301/26, 26-May-2026, Cuadro 5).

unemployment.source: INEGI, ENOE, Tasa de Desocupacion (% de la PEA) por entidad federativa (Boletin de Indicador 301/26, Cuadro 5).

meta.notes: HONESTY: every value is from the official primary source cited; no value is estimated except gdp_per_capita (a transparent division of two sourced numbers) and gdp_per_capita_usd (fixed 17.5 FX). YEARS ARE MIXED BY DESIGN (latest available per metric): GDP 2023, population 2023, poverty 2022, labor Q1-2026. GDP is NOMINAL (valores corrientes); note the INEGI PIBE press-release Cuadro 1 is instead in constant-2018 prices, which is a different (smaller) series - not used here. Each state's nominal GDP was read from INEGI's per-entity PIBE-2023 boletin ('PIB nominal de X millones de pesos'); the 32 values sum to exactly 31,855,566 million pesos, the published national total (internal check passed). Population uses CONAPO reconciled mid-2023 projections, which run ~1.7% above the raw Censo 2020 count (national CONAPO 2020 = 128,209,170 vs Censo 2020 = 126,014,024) because CONAPO corrects for census undercount; CONAPO is the standard denominator for per-capita. Per-capita ordering sanity check PASSED: Campeche highest (629,318 MXN / $35,961, oil), then CDMX, Nuevo Leon, Coahuila, Sonora; Chiapas lowest (83,697 MXN / $4,783), with Guerrero, Oaxaca, Tlaxcala near the bottom. DATA MEXICO CUBE: could not be verified - the live Data Mexico host (www.economia.gob.mx/apidatamexico and its datamexico.org redirect) was unreachable from this environment (connection timeout/block); the api.datamexico.org and dev.datamexico.org hosts no longer resolve (DNS). Confirmed from Data Mexico docs: base endpoint https://www.economia.gob.mx/apidatamexico/tesseract/data.jsonrecords, drilldown 'State', and one working cube 'coneval_poverty' (measures Population, Poverty, Extreme Poverty, Moderate Poverty). The GDP/PIB cube name+measure could NOT be confirmed and must be read from the live Tesseract UI (economia.gob.mx/datamexico -> 'Ver datos') before wiring; not guessed here.

30.name: Veracruz de Ignacio de la Llave
